description:
Cargo.toml inherits workspace.rust-version = "1.75" but cargo +1.75 check -p secretx-gcp-sm fails because the dep tree pulls hashbrown 0.17.0 (requires edition2024, stabilized in Rust 1.85). Path: secretx-gcp-sm -> reqwest 0.13 -> hyper 1 -> h2 -> indexmap 2.14 -> hashbrown 0.17. A user attempting to build at the stated MSRV gets a parse error on hashbrown's manifest, not a useful message.

Cross-crate note: this is a workspace-wide issue (every crate that pulls reqwest is affected). Fix is workspace-level: either bump rust-version to 1.85, or pin indexmap < 2.14 / hashbrown < 0.17 transitively, or document the discrepancy. Filing under this crate's epic per slash-command rule but the resolution must touch the workspace Cargo.toml.

Trigger: cargo +1.75 check -p secretx-gcp-sm
Actual: 'feature edition2024 is required' parse error on hashbrown-0.17.0/Cargo.toml.
Expected: clean build, since rust-version = 1.75 is a public claim users will rely on.
notes: Verified: actual minimum is 1.86 (not 1.85). 1.85 fails on icu_provider@2.2.0 requiring 1.86. Affected crates: secretx-gcp-sm, secretx-doppler, secretx-hashicorp-vault (all pull reqwest), secretx-k8s (pulls kube→hyper). Local-only crates (env, file, local-signing, cache, keyring, pkcs11, wolfhsm) likely still build at 1.75. Options: (A) bump workspace MSRV to 1.86, (B) split MSRV per-crate with rust-version overrides, (C) pin transitive deps. Recommend A — simplest and most honest.
title: MSRV 1.75 cannot build: hashbrown 0.17 needs edition2024
